Description:
Ballistics of the 125mm Step-2 Tungsten APFSDS round for use on weapons of the D-81 family, including the ZPT-98-125. 

The performance target for the second phase of Project 123 was intended to be met with a TEGDN (太根) based charge design, propelling the second phase APFSDS projectile. However, during development, it was realized that the TEGDN under consideration suffers from poor low temperature mechanical strength. Consequently, this charge design utilizing high-nitration nitrocellulose was developed in parallel, as a backup. It ultimately proved unnecessary, and was therefore not adopted.

Projectile: 125-2 7.44 kg
Charge: 9.24 kg High Nitration Nitrocellulose (1046.4 kJ/kg computed, 1029.7 kJ/kg measured)
- Main:
	- 6.00 kg 16/19 Rosette
- Incremental:		3.24 kg
	- 2.54 kg 16/19 Rosette
	- 0.7  kg 19/1-30
- Combustible Cartridge: 
	- 1.12 kg TNT impregnated nitrocellulose
		- 754 kJ/kg (as computed)
		- 686.5 kJ/kg (as measured)
		- Alternatively given as 588 kJ/kg
Load density of 0.83 g/cc.

As tested: [copper crusher gauge]
Temp	Pmcp	Velocity
°C	MPa	m/s
-40	439.1	1678.1
 15	485.2	1727.9
 50	519.3	1770.3

Reference:
- 王建政《高氮量单基发射药用于穿甲弹（二步）的装药设计与研究》, 兵器工业集团第三事业部
- 郑泽义, 田书春《125mm坦克炮穿甲弹装药结构设计特点与分析》, 国营第845厂
- 滑俊生，郑泽义《高膛压下装药结构的研究和发展》，国营845厂
